domains.description: Stable machine-readable codes emitted by script edit and validation flows.

entries.meaning: A required edit field was omitted or could not be normalized.
entries.typical_fix: Send the required path, range, or replacement fields.

entries.meaning: Explicit line or column coordinates were sent as 0-based values.
entries.typical_fix: Resend explicit line and column coordinates as 1-based indices.

entries.meaning: Two requested edit spans overlap.
entries.typical_fix: Split or reorder edits so no two spans intersect.

entries.meaning: The server could not build the requested local preview payload.
entries.typical_fix: Retry with a smaller edit batch or remove preview mode.

entries.meaning: A path or URI resolved outside the Unity Assets folder.
entries.typical_fix: Use an asset path rooted under Assets/.

entries.meaning: The provided path was malformed, absolute, or traversal-like.
entries.typical_fix: Send a normalized Unity asset path without traversal segments.

entries.meaning: The provided file extension is not valid for that script operation.
entries.typical_fix: Use a .cs target path for script creation.

entries.meaning: The requested script validation level is not recognized.
entries.typical_fix: Use one of the documented level values.

entries.meaning: An anchor-based edit target could not be resolved.
entries.typical_fix: Re-read the file and provide a more specific anchor.

entries.meaning: The requested structured edit operation is not supported.
entries.typical_fix: Rewrite the request using a supported edit op.

entries.meaning: No applicable text spans were computed for the requested edit.
entries.typical_fix: Verify that the target method, class, or anchor still exists.

entries.meaning: The server failed converting a higher-level edit into raw spans.
entries.typical_fix: Simplify the edit request and retry after re-reading the file.

entries.meaning: The content SHA256 did not match, indicating concurrent modification.
entries.typical_fix: Re-read the file and retry the operation with the updated SHA.

entries.meaning: The target script file could not be found.
entries.typical_fix: Verify the file path exists and is correctly spelled.

entries.meaning: The target file is locked by another process (e.g., IDE, version control).
entries.typical_fix: Close the file in other applications and retry.

entries.meaning: The resulting script would have syntax errors.
entries.typical_fix: Review the edit for unbalanced braces, missing semicolons, etc.

domains.title: Script Editing

domains.description: Stable Unity-side error codes emitted by ScriptableObject management.

entries.meaning: Unity was compiling or reloading and rejected the write operation.
entries.typical_fix: Wait until the editor is idle, then retry.

entries.meaning: A required ScriptableObject parameter was missing or malformed.
entries.typical_fix: Validate the action payload and resend the required fields.

entries.meaning: The requested ScriptableObject CLR type could not be resolved.
entries.typical_fix: Use a valid namespace-qualified ScriptableObject type name.

entries.meaning: The target folder path is invalid or outside the supported project area.
entries.typical_fix: Use a valid folder rooted under Assets/.

entries.meaning: The ScriptableObject target could not be resolved from guid or path.
entries.typical_fix: Re-resolve the asset guid or path and retry.

entries.meaning: Unity failed while creating or saving the requested asset.
entries.typical_fix: Inspect the payload and target path, then retry after Unity is idle.

entries.meaning: Unity failed while modifying the ScriptableObject.
entries.typical_fix: Check that all serialized fields exist and types match.

entries.meaning: The provided value type does not match the expected field type.
entries.typical_fix: Ensure JSON values match the C# field types (e.g., use numbers for int/float).

domains.title: Scriptable Objects

domains.description: Error codes for GameObject CRUD operations.

entries.meaning: The target GameObject could not be found by name, path, or ID.
entries.typical_fix: Verify the GameObject exists in the current scene. Use find_gameobjects to search.

entries.meaning: The specified parent GameObject is invalid or not found.
entries.typical_fix: Verify the parent exists, or use null for root-level objects.

entries.meaning: The requested component could not be found on the target GameObject.
entries.typical_fix: Verify the component name is correct and exists on the GameObject.

entries.meaning: Failed to add the component (type may not exist or be allowed).
entries.typical_fix: Verify the component type name is fully qualified and the assembly is loaded.

entries.meaning: The provided position, rotation, or scale values are invalid.
entries.typical_fix: Use valid numeric arrays with 3 elements [x, y, z].

domains.title: GameObject Management

entries.meaning: The requested scene could not be found.
entries.typical_fix: Verify the scene path is correct and the scene exists in the project.

entries.meaning: Unity failed to load the scene.
entries.typical_fix: Check that the scene is in the build settings and has no load errors.

entries.meaning: Unity failed to save the scene.
entries.typical_fix: Check file permissions and disk space.

entries.meaning: The requested scene is already the active scene.
entries.typical_fix: No action needed, or specify a different scene to activate.

entries.meaning: The prefab asset could not be found.
entries.typical_fix: Verify the prefab path is correct and exists in the project.

entries.meaning: Failed to instantiate the prefab into the scene.
entries.typical_fix: Verify the prefab is valid and can be instantiated.

entries.meaning: The operation is not supported on prefab variants.
entries.typical_fix: Apply the operation to the base prefab or use supported actions.

entries.meaning: Attempted to modify a nested prefab which requires special handling.
entries.typical_fix: Unpack or open the prefab for editing before making changes.

entries.meaning: The asset could not be found at the specified path.
entries.typical_fix: Verify the asset path is correct and the asset exists.

entries.meaning: Unity failed to import the asset.
entries.typical_fix: Check the asset file format and integrity.

entries.meaning: The provided GUID is invalid or malformed.
entries.typical_fix: Use a valid Unity GUID (32 hexadecimal characters).

entries.meaning: No asset found with the specified GUID.
entries.typical_fix: Verify the GUID is correct and the asset exists in the project.

entries.meaning: The batch contains more commands than the configured maximum.
entries.typical_fix: Split the batch into smaller chunks or increase the limit in Unity settings.

entries.meaning: One or more commands in the batch are malformed or missing required fields.
entries.typical_fix: Ensure each command has 'tool' and 'params' fields with valid values.

entries.meaning: Some commands in the batch succeeded while others failed.
entries.typical_fix: Review the per-command results and retry failed commands individually.

entries.meaning: Commands in the batch have circular dependencies.
entries.typical_fix: Restructure the batch to remove circular dependencies.

domains.description: Error codes for Unity connection issues.

entries.meaning: No Unity instance is currently connected.
entries.typical_fix: Ensure Unity is running with the MCP plugin enabled.

entries.meaning: The connection to Unity timed out.
entries.typical_fix: Check Unity is responsive and retry the operation.

entries.meaning: The specified Unity instance could not be found.
entries.typical_fix: List available instances with mcpforunity://instances and use a valid Name@hash.

entries.meaning: Failed to serialize or deserialize the request/response.
entries.typical_fix: Check that all parameters are JSON-serializable.

domains.title: Connection and Transport

domains.description: Error codes for capability and permission issues.

entries.meaning: The tool is disabled by configuration or group settings.
entries.typical_fix: Enable the tool group with manage_tools(action='activate') or check capability config.

entries.meaning: This tool requires explicit user opt-in before use.
entries.typical_fix: Add the tool to the tool_opt_in section of capabilities.json.

entries.meaning: This tool only works when Unity is in play mode.
entries.typical_fix: Enter play mode in Unity before using this tool.

entries.meaning: The requested dry-run (preview) mode is not supported by this tool.
entries.typical_fix: Remove the dry_run flag or use a different tool.

domains.title: Tool Capability Errors

operational_patterns.meaning: No active Unity instance is selected for the current session.
operational_patterns.pattern: success=false with guidance to call set_active_instance
operational_patterns.typical_fix: Call set_active_instance with a Name@hash value from mcpforunity://instances.

operational_patterns.meaning: Unity is compiling, importing, running tests, or otherwise not ready for mutation.
operational_patterns.pattern: preflight-gated tool returns success=false before mutation
operational_patterns.typical_fix: Wait for the editor to become ready, then retry the mutation tool.

operational_patterns.meaning: The connected Unity plugin is too old to report tool-state visibility.
operational_patterns.typical_fix: Update the Unity package or toggle groups manually with activate/deactivate.

operational_patterns.meaning: The server is temporarily unable to process the request (compiling, testing, etc.).
operational_patterns.typical_fix: Wait for the specified retry_after_ms period and retry the request.

operational_patterns.meaning: A recoverable error occurred with machine-readable details.
operational_patterns.pattern: success=false with code and data fields
operational_patterns.typical_fix: Check the 'code' field against the error catalog and apply the typical fix.
